title: UX Design Studio POC
description: Spec-driven visual preview and per-screen approval workbench concept extension for InsaneSDD 2.0.

project.title: UX Design Studio POC
project.description: 50-hour UX Design Studio proof-of-work backlog and delivery board.

milestone.title: v0.1.0 POC
milestone.description: UX Design Studio interview POC, capped at 50 build hours.

definitionOfReady: UXSpec input and required registry types are identified.
Mandatory or cut-line classification is explicit.
definitionOfDone: Relevant unit, contract, integration, accessibility, lint, type and build checks pass.
No frozen Source of Truth, PRD or architecture rule is weakened.
PR references its issue and targets staging.
Documentation and screenshots are updated when applicable.

hardGates.name: Plan and architecture baseline
hardGates.passCondition: Source of Truth, PRD, architecture, development plan, ticket structure and cut-line are approved before implementation.

hardGates.name: Rendering walking skeleton
hardGates.passCondition: All five screens render through one validated registry/composer path with runtime tokens and generated navigation.

hardGates.name: Governance end to end
hardGates.passCondition: Approval, revision, role policy, append-only audit and valid reload persistence work together.

hardGates.name: Version-bound regeneration
hardGates.passCondition: Mock regeneration creates a validated new current version and prior approval does not approve it.

hardGates.name: Release-ready demo
hardGates.passCondition: CI, production build, static deployment, accessibility checks, reset flow, complete demo and release acceptance pass.

labels.name: uxds:managed
labels.description: Managed by the UXDS backlog importer

epics.title: Application Foundation and UXSpec Contracts
epics.objective: Establish the reproducible SPA foundation, immutable domain contracts, runtime validation, and AgentPilot seed baseline.

stories.title: Bootstrap the frontend engineering foundation
stories.userStory: As an engineer, I need a strict, reproducible React and TypeScript workspace so that every later capability is built and verified consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Vite, React 18, strict TypeScript, React Router, CSS Modules, Vitest, and React Testing Library are configured.
The application exposes /overview, /review/:screenId, and /audit routes.
Lint, type-check, test, build, and preview commands are documented and executable.
Application and route error boundaries prevent an unhandled shell failure.

tasks.title: Initialize Vite, React, TypeScript and quality tooling
tasks.outcome: A reproducible pnpm workspace with strict compiler and quality checks.
acceptanceCriteria: One lockfile is used.
Strict TypeScript is enabled.
Core scripts execute from a clean install.

tasks.title: Create application shell, routes and root boundaries
tasks.outcome: A navigable shell with isolated route failure handling.
acceptanceCriteria: All required routes resolve.
Unknown routes return a controlled fallback.
The shell remains available after a feature error.

stories.title: Define and validate immutable UXSpec contracts
stories.userStory: As the rendering system, I need a typed and runtime-validated UX specification so that generated data cannot bypass domain and safety constraints.
acceptanceCriteria: UXSpec, ScreenSpec, ComponentNode, Persona, Journey, DesignTokens, navigation, responsive, and accessibility contracts are defined.
Runtime schemas validate raw specification data and reject unsafe or malformed values.
Cross-reference invariants verify unique IDs and valid persona, journey, navigation, and screen references.
Validated source data is exposed as readonly and is not used to store governance state.

tasks.title: Implement UXSpec TypeScript models and runtime schemas
tasks.outcome: Compile-time and runtime contracts for every required UXSpec entity.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema and model names stay aligned.
Unknown executable content is not accepted.
Safe defaults are explicit.

tasks.title: Implement normalization, invariants and specification loader
tasks.outcome: A single validated readonly input boundary for the application.
acceptanceCriteria: Duplicate IDs fail validation.
Broken references fail with diagnostics.
Valid documents become readonly application state.

stories.title: Author and verify the AgentPilot seed baseline
stories.userStory: As a reviewer, I need the POC to use a faithful AgentPilot UXSpec so that the demo represents the same personas, journeys, screens, navigation, and design system shown in the source workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Seed data contains three personas, three journeys, five screens, navigation rules, design tokens, accessibility metadata, and approximately 30 composed nodes.
Dashboard and Login are authored first, followed by Task Detail, Workflow Templates, and Reports Export.
The seed passes the same runtime validation used for future API or AI input.
No screen depends on a page-specific React implementation.

tasks.title: Create AgentPilot personas, journeys, tokens and navigation seed
tasks.outcome: A validated shared baseline containing non-screen UX context.
acceptanceCriteria: Alex, Jordan, and Taylor are present.
Navigation reflects desktop sidebar and mobile compact behavior.
Baseline and spec versions are explicit.

tasks.title: Create five screen trees and seed contract tests
tasks.outcome: Five valid screen definitions ready for the common renderer.
acceptanceCriteria: All required screens exist.
Every node ID is unique per screen.
Every known node type maps to the planned registry.

epics.title: Spec-Driven Rendering and Runtime Theming
epics.objective: Prove the highest-risk technical capability: safely compose every screen through one renderer and apply one runtime token source.

stories.title: Implement the allowlisted component registry
stories.userStory: As the composer, I need an immutable registry of reusable primitives so that UXSpec nodes resolve safely without arbitrary imports or executable content.
acceptanceCriteria: Approximately 12 to 15 component types cover layout, forms, navigation, data display, feedback, and chart placeholders.
Every registry entry owns a prop validator and declares whether it accepts children.
Unknown types and invalid props return accessible fallbacks.
Registry components consume render context and scoped tokens without importing governance state.

tasks.title: Define registry contracts and core layout, content and form primitives
tasks.outcome: A typed immutable registry with the foundational reusable component set.
acceptanceCriteria: Registry lookup is O(1).
Each entry has a validator.
Components do not evaluate spec-provided code.

tasks.title: Add navigation, data, feedback and chart primitives with fallbacks
tasks.outcome: The complete capped registry required by all five seed screens.
acceptanceCriteria: The registry stays within the cap.
Unsupported nodes remain visible and accessible.
All seed types resolve.

stories.title: Implement recursive composition and safe actions
stories.userStory: As a reviewer, I need every selected screen recursively composed from UXSpec data so that the POC proves a single rendering path rather than hard-coded pages.
acceptanceCriteria: The composer recursively renders nested children through registry lookup.
A depth guard, node boundary, invalid-prop placeholder, and unknown-type placeholder isolate failures.
Only declarative allowlisted actions such as navigate, open dialog, submit demo form, and no-op are supported.
All five screens render without page-specific branches.

tasks.title: Build recursive composer, render context and node isolation
tasks.outcome: A safe recursive screen renderer with bounded failure scope.
acceptanceCriteria: Maximum depth is enforced.
Stable node IDs are used as keys.
One node failure does not blank the screen.

tasks.title: Implement declarative action resolver and renderer tests
tasks.outcome: Controlled screen interactions with verified renderer invariants.
acceptanceCriteria: Unknown actions become safe no-ops.
Known navigation actions resolve.
Nested, invalid and unknown nodes are covered by tests.

stories.title: Apply safe runtime design tokens
stories.userStory: As a design reviewer, I need specification tokens to reskin the preview instantly so that visual consistency can be evaluated across every screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Semantic color, typography, spacing, radius, and layout tokens map to --uxds-* CSS variables.
Token values are allowlisted and validated before assignment.
Tokens are scoped to the preview root and never restyle the studio shell.
A preview token override updates the active screen without page reload or source mutation.

tasks.title: Implement semantic token validation and CSS variable mapper
tasks.outcome: A safe deterministic token-to-variable boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Arbitrary CSS keys are rejected.
Unsafe url or expression values are rejected.
Every required token category maps.

tasks.title: Apply scoped theme and live preview overrides
tasks.outcome: One effective token source visibly updates all registered components.
acceptanceCriteria: The studio chrome is unaffected.
Overrides do not mutate UXSpec.
Token updates occur without reload.

stories.title: Render five screens with generated navigation
stories.userStory: As a Product Owner, I need to move through all five rendered AgentPilot screens so that complete product flow can be evaluated before Agile planning.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard, Task Detail, Workflow Templates, Reports Export, and Login render through the same composer.
Desktop navigation uses the seeded sidebar model and mobile uses the compact model.
Valid generated navigation changes the /review/:screenId route and identifies the active destination.
Screen switching feels immediate and does not reload the application.

tasks.title: Complete screen composition fidelity and responsive node rules
tasks.outcome: All five wireframes render with intentional structure, content, states and reusable primitives.
acceptanceCriteria: No hard-coded screen component is introduced.
Required data, forms, feedback and charts are represented.
Desktop and mobile rules are present.

tasks.title: Integrate generated navigation, routing and rendering gate tests
tasks.outcome: A complete navigable walking skeleton and evidence for Hard Gate 2.
acceptanceCriteria: Every valid navigation target opens.
The active screen is highlighted.
A contract test proves all screens use the registry and composer.

epics.title: Review Workbench and Contextual Lenses
epics.objective: Turn the renderer into a review experience with overview, persona, responsive, and optional journey context.

stories.title: Deliver specification overview and review workbench
stories.userStory: As a Product Owner, I need a summary and screen review workspace so that I can understand the UXSpec and enter any screen's decision flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Overview displays three personas, three journeys, five screens, component count, design-system summary, and approval progress placeholder.
Every screen card exposes its current review status and opens the review route.
Workbench separates screen navigation, preview canvas, lens controls, and decision panel.
Loading, empty, partial, and route error states preserve the application shell.

tasks.title: Build UXSpec overview and screen status entry points
tasks.outcome: A concise project-level summary with direct screen access.
acceptanceCriteria: All required counts are derived from seed data.
Each screen is selectable.
Status presentation accepts governance selectors.

tasks.title: Build screen review workbench layout and shell states
tasks.outcome: A stable workspace around the renderer.
acceptanceCriteria: Preview and studio controls are visually distinct.
Core regions are keyboard reachable.
Failure states do not obscure navigation.

stories.title: Add persona review lens
stories.userStory: As a stakeholder, I need to review a screen as Alex, Jordan, or Taylor so that goals, frustrations, and journey touchpoints inform the decision.
acceptanceCriteria: Alex, Jordan, and Taylor can be selected.
Current-screen goals, frustrations, and touchpoints are derived from UXSpec.
Annotations appear in a side panel or separate layer and do not mutate component props.
The underlying preview remains pointer and keyboard accessible.

tasks.title: Implement persona selectors and current-screen context resolution
tasks.outcome: Deterministic persona context derived from the immutable specification.
acceptanceCriteria: Selection is ephemeral UI state.
Missing touchpoints produce a clear empty state.
No UXSpec mutation occurs.

tasks.title: Build accessible persona lens presentation
tasks.outcome: A review annotation layer distinct from the product wireframe.
acceptanceCriteria: The selected persona is announced.
Annotations remain readable at required breakpoints.
Preview interaction remains available.

stories.title: Add responsive preview and optional journey walkthrough
stories.userStory: As a design reviewer, I need mobile, tablet, and desktop simulation, with an optional guided journey, so that layout and flow can be evaluated without page reload.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile, tablet, and desktop widths update the preview frame and render context.
Mobile stacks and larger breakpoints use seeded multi-column rules.
One guided journey can move forward and backward through related screens while in scope.
Journey is Cut 1 and tablet is Cut 4, each removable without changing the renderer.

tasks.title: Implement breakpoint preview context and recomposition
tasks.outcome: Responsive screen review across the required device modes.
acceptanceCriteria: No iframe or page reload is required.
Mobile and desktop remain mandatory.
Tablet behavior is isolated by configuration.

tasks.title: Implement isolated guided journey module and feature flags
tasks.outcome: A delete-safe optional walkthrough controlled by the cut-line configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: One seeded journey is navigable.
Current step is visible.
Disabling the flag removes the feature without core changes.

epics.title: Screen-Level Governance, Roles and Audit
epics.objective: Deliver version-bound approvals, structured revisions, append-only audit truth, browser recovery, and mocked role enforcement.

stories.title: Implement event-driven governance domain
stories.userStory: As the application, I need pure commands, events, reducer transitions, and selectors so that visible status and audit evidence cannot diverge.
acceptanceCriteria: Approval, revision-request, regeneration-started, regenerated, and regeneration-failed events share stable baseline and version metadata.
Reducer operations are pure, deterministic, immutable, and reject duplicate event IDs.
Status, current screen version, progress, and gate completion are derived through selectors.
Clock and ID generation are injected outside the reducer.

tasks.title: Define governance commands, events, policies and reducer
tasks.outcome: A stable append-only governance contract independent of React and browser APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: Every event identifies project, spec, baseline, screen, version, actor and time.
Duplicate IDs are rejected.
State updates are immutable.

tasks.title: Implement current-version status and gate selectors with unit tests
tasks.outcome: One derivation path for review status, progress and gate truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Later revision or regeneration invalidates prior effective approval.
Other screens remain unchanged.
Gate completes only for all current versions.

stories.title: Approve current screen versions and complete the UX gate
stories.userStory: As an Approver, I need to approve one current screen version independently so that accepted work remains traceable and the gate completes only at the correct time.
acceptanceCriteria: Approval applies only to the active screen and version.
Actor, action, screen, spec, baseline, version, timestamp, and optional comment are recorded.
Overview and workbench update immediately without affecting other screens.
The final approval displays readiness for Agile plan generation.

tasks.title: Implement approve-screen use case and decision UI
tasks.outcome: A validated application command and accessible approval interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Stale versions cannot be approved.
Only the active screen changes.
The event is appended exactly once.

tasks.title: Integrate progress and final gate-completion presentation
tasks.outcome: Consistent screen and project-level approval feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Remaining-screen count is correct.
Completion is unavailable early.
Completion is announced after the final approval.

stories.title: Request structured revisions with role enforcement
stories.userStory: As an Approver, I need to submit component-specific revision evidence, while Reviewer and Viewer remain read-only, so that feedback is actionable and the permission model is demonstrable.
acceptanceCriteria: Revision form captures affected node IDs, category, and required description.
The request references active screen, screen version, spec version, baseline, actor, and timestamp.
Approver can approve, revise, and regenerate; Reviewer and Viewer cannot invoke those commands.
The role switcher is explicitly identified as a POC demonstration mechanism.

tasks.title: Build revision form, validation and request use case
tasks.outcome: Structured screen-level feedback recorded as a governance event.
acceptanceCriteria: Required fields block invalid submission.
Affected nodes resolve to the active screen.
The event appears in screen history.

tasks.title: Implement role capabilities and dual-layer enforcement
tasks.outcome: Mocked role behavior enforced in both UI and application commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Restricted actions are hidden or disabled.
Command bypass attempts are rejected.
All roles can view allowed content.

stories.title: Persist governance state and expose the audit log
stories.userStory: As a reviewer, I need governance history to survive reload and remain inspectable chronologically so that the POC demonstrates traceable decisions against the frozen baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: A versioned localStorage repository persists only governance events and screen versions.
Stored data is validated against project, spec, and baseline before rehydration.
Corrupted or unavailable storage degrades to clean in-memory state with a visible notice.
Audit view lists events chronologically and supports per-screen inspection and reset-demo-state.

tasks.title: Implement versioned localStorage repository and recovery path
tasks.outcome: Validated, bounded browser persistence isolated behind a port.
acceptanceCriteria: Valid state round-trips.
Mismatched or corrupt data is rejected.
Storage failure does not prevent use.

tasks.title: Build chronological audit view, filters and reset action
tasks.outcome: Persistent governance evidence suitable for the demo flow.
acceptanceCriteria: All required event metadata is shown.
Per-screen filtering works.
Reset removes only the managed storage key.

epics.title: Mock Regeneration and Review Hardening
epics.objective: Demonstrate replaceable AI regeneration, version invalidation, accessibility evidence, studio states, and InsaneSDD-aligned polish.

stories.title: Regenerate one screen through the provider boundary
stories.userStory: As an Approver, I need a structured revision to produce a deterministic new screen version through DesignAgentProvider so that future AI replacement is proven without external service risk.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI depends only on DesignAgentProvider, not the mock implementation.
Mock provider returns a deterministic pre-authored variant after visible simulated latency.
Provider result passes the same runtime screen validation before activation.
Regeneration can be cancelled, can show one controlled failure, and never requires a real LLM.

tasks.title: Define provider port and deterministic mock adapter
tasks.outcome: A transport-agnostic regeneration boundary with pre-authored variants.
acceptanceCriteria: Request carries baseline, version, revision and optional persona context.
AbortSignal is honored.
No network or secret is required.

tasks.title: Implement regeneration use case, loading, validation and failure flow
tasks.outcome: A complete safe mocked regeneration sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: Start and result events are appended.
Invalid output is not activated.
Current version remains intact on failure.

stories.title: Enforce reapproval and finish accessibility and studio states
stories.userStory: As a reviewer, I need regenerated output to require fresh approval and the studio to communicate accessibility, loading, empty, error, and partial states clearly so that the demo is trustworthy.
acceptanceCriteria: Regeneration creates a new current version and any prior-version approval does not approve it.
A minimal prior/current version comparison remains while version-history UI is in scope.
Accessibility overlay presents seeded contrast, ARIA, screen-reader, and keyboard notes; contrast badges remain if the overlay is cut.
Studio controls meet the primary WCAG 2.1 AA keyboard, focus, naming, live-region, and reduced-motion expectations.
Visual treatment aligns with the demonstrated light chrome, red accent, and sidebar language without claiming pixel-perfect fidelity.

tasks.title: Implement version activation, reapproval rule and optional history panel
tasks.outcome: Correct version-bound governance and evidence for Hard Gate 4.
acceptanceCriteria: New version status is ready for review.
Old approval remains historical only.
History UI is isolated as Cut 2.

tasks.title: Implement accessibility evidence, resilient states and visual polish
tasks.outcome: A presentable, keyboard-usable and failure-aware review experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Full overlay is Cut 3 while contrast badges remain.
Focus and live announcements work on critical flow.
All required UI states are demonstrated.

epics.title: Quality, Documentation, Deployment and Demonstration
epics.objective: Produce verifiable engineering evidence, reproducible delivery, static deployment, release traceability, and a rehearsed interview workflow.

stories.title: Automate critical quality gates and CI
stories.userStory: As an engineering lead, I need targeted automated evidence so that renderer safety, governance correctness, persistence, provider contracts, and the production build are continuously verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Renderer unit tests cover known, nested, unknown, invalid, depth-guard, and declarative-action cases.
Governance tests cover independence, duplicate events, revision invalidation, regeneration, reapproval, gate completion, role rejection, and chronology.
Contract tests validate seed, registry coverage, provider output, and persistence round trips.
One critical RTL integration flow covers overview to review, revision, regeneration, approval, reload, and role restriction.
CI runs install, lint, type-check, tests, and production build for pull requests targeting staging and main.

tasks.title: Complete unit, contract and critical integration test suite
tasks.outcome: Focused regression evidence for the core technical and product proof.
acceptanceCriteria: Mandatory invariants are asserted directly.
No broad snapshot or pixel regression scope is added.
Tests run deterministically offline.

tasks.title: Configure minimal GitHub Actions quality pipeline
tasks.outcome: Repeatable checks for staging and release pull requests.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm lockfile is honored.
Failures block the quality gate.
The production build artifact is reproducible.

stories.title: Document, deploy, rehearse and release the POC
stories.userStory: As an evaluator, I need a deployed, explainable and correctly positioned artifact so that product judgment and Lead Frontend engineering can be reviewed end to end.
acceptanceCriteria: README explains setup, architecture, demo reset, scope, exclusions, concept-extension positioning, and production path.
ADR records accepted architecture decisions and explicitly excludes real AI, backend, auth, and Module Federation.
Static deployment works through direct and deep-linked routes without secrets or external services.
The complete demo flow is rehearsed, timed, and validated against release acceptance.
All feature work merges to staging by squash PR; final staging to main release PR is tagged v0.1.0-poc.

tasks.title: Complete README, ADR, traceability and deployment configuration
tasks.outcome: A self-contained repository and static preview suitable for interview review.
acceptanceCriteria: Concept-extension wording is explicit.
PRD-to-issue-to-test evidence is documented.
Direct route refresh works.

tasks.title: Run release acceptance, demo rehearsal and staging-to-main release
tasks.outcome: A stable presentation build and controlled v0.1.0-poc release.
acceptanceCriteria: Reset and prepared demo states are verified.
All hard gates pass.
Cut-line decisions are recorded.
Release PR targets main from staging.
